choices.text: Tailwind's JIT compiler scans your source files for FULL class strings at build time. It can't see partially-constructed strings like `bg-${color}-500` — the literal `bg-red-500` never appears in source, so Tailwind never generates that utility. Fix: use full class names in your logic: `const colorClass = color === 'red' ? 'bg-red-500' : 'bg-blue-500'` (both full strings are visible to the scanner). For truly dynamic combinations, add classes to the `safelist` in tailwind.config. Tailwind doesn't generate utilities at runtime — only at build time from what it can statically see.

questions.explanation: Tailwind scans for full class strings at build. Runtime-built strings are invisible; use complete classnames in logic.

questions.prompt: Tailwind class order on a button feels arbitrary across files. Best practice?

choices.text: Install `prettier-plugin-tailwindcss`. It auto-sorts classes to Tailwind's canonical order on save/format: layout → spacing → sizing → typography → colors → borders → effects → states. Eliminates bike-shedding over class order in code review, keeps diffs clean (manual edits don't cause spurious reordering), and gives every file a predictable shape. Tailwind itself doesn't care about order; the plugin is purely for human readability and consistency. Standard part of the modern Tailwind setup.

questions.explanation: prettier-plugin-tailwindcss auto-sorts to canonical order. Eliminates order debates.
